publications.title: Broad CD8+ T cell cross-recognition of distinct influenza A strains in humans.
publications.authors: Grant EJ, Josephs TM, Loh L, Clemens EB, Sant S, Bharadwaj M, Chen W, Rossjohn J, Gras S, Kedzierska K.
publications.executive_summary: This study examined TCR cross-reactivity against influenza A virus (IAV) variants in humans, focusing on two immunodominant CD8+ T-cell epitopes: the HLA-B*37:01-restricted NP338–346 peptide and the HLA-A*01:01-restricted NP44–52 peptide. Crystal structures of wild-type and variant peptides, together with a cross-reactive TCR–HLA-B37–NP338 complex structure, revealed that TCRαβ clonotypes recognize distinct IAV strains through the structurally conserved conformation of the epitope variants, enabling broad CD8+ T-cell cross-recognition despite sequence mutations. This finding is significant for influenza vaccine design, as it suggests that CD8+ T-cell-targeted vaccines could provide protection across genetically divergent IAV strains, offering an advantage when vaccine strains are mismatched to circulating viruses or novel pandemic strains emerge with limited antibody-mediated immunity.
